labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

body:
Perhaps this is related to those weird MultiRegion-related methods appearing with Distributed? See https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaOcean.jl/issues/647#issue-3485830061

Since the method wasn't imported, the attempt below to extent it might resulted in overwriting it?

What was missing was:
```julia
import Oceananigans.BoundaryConditions: FieldBoundaryConditions
```

Julia v1.12 warned me for that; that's how I found it.

```Julia
  1 dependency had output during precompilation:
┌ Oceananigans
│  WARNING: Constructor for type "FieldBoundaryConditions" was extended in `MultiRegion` without explicit qualification or import.
│    NOTE: Assumed "FieldBoundaryConditions" refers to `BoundaryConditions.FieldBoundaryConditions`. This behavior is deprecated and may differ in future versions.`
│    NOTE: This behavior may have differed in Julia versions prior to 1.12.
│    Hint: If you intended to create a new generic function of the same name, use `function FieldBoundaryConditions end`.
│    Hint: To silence the warning, qualify `FieldBoundaryConditions` as `BoundaryConditions.FieldBoundaryConditions` in the method signature or explicitly `import BoundaryConditions: FieldBoundaryConditions`.
└
```

cc @taimoorsohail
title: Add missing import when extending `FieldBoundaryConditions` in `MultiRegion`

body:
Keyword arguments were thrown away in the distributed zipper, causing it to fill distributed halos also when `only_local_halo = false`. As a consequence, MPI transfers were also synchronized in "local" fill halos, destroying the computation - communication overlap.
This PR fixes this behavior. Correctness does not change, only performance.

cc @xkykai @taimoorsohail

labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster
labels.name: performance 🏍️

labels.description: Our plan for total cluster domination
labels.name: distributed 🕸️

title: Pass correct `kwargs...` in distributed tripolar `fill_halo_event!`

body:
- [x] Further cleanup of:
  https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/cbfb6f24fd0c6852e730f49904ed8b27c6d033ee/ext/OceananigansXESMFExt.jl#L12-L28
  eg use `permutedims` instead of `'`
- [x] Does the regridding reproduces the Python xESMF regridding?
- [x] (Perhaps related to the above) Can the integral before and after regridding be better than `rtol = 1e-4`?
- [x] Documentation/Example for how to do a regridding using XESMF.jl (docstrings with examples should suffice for now; convert some example(s) to doctest(s)).


Closes #4818
title: Docs, Examples, and Validation for the XESMF extension
